advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Make ACLI_SHA256 verification mandatory (fail if unset) rather than an optional warning path.
evidence: The binary is then chmod +x'd, added to /usr/local/bin, and run with JIRA_API_TOKEN piped to it — an unverified binary handling secrets.
advisory.finding: The acli binary is downloaded from acli.atlassian.com and installed/executed with Jira credentials while checksum verification is only optional — when ACLI_SHA256 is unset the workflow explicitly proceeds without any integrity check, defeating the pinning intent.
scenarios: Compromised or tampered acli tarball is downloaded and executed with live Jira credentials because ACLI_SHA256 was never configured.
advisory.suggested_fix: Treat a missing ACLI_SHA256 as a hard error like ACLI_VERSION: exit 1 with an ::error:: when the digest is unset, so the acli binary is never installed unverified.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Pin actions/cache to a full commit SHA (with a version comment) instead of the @v4 tag.
advisory.finding: The third-party GitHub Action actions/cache is pinned to a mutable major-version tag (@v4) rather than a full commit SHA, leaving the workflow's action supply chain unpinned.
scenarios: A compromised or retagged v4 release runs in a job that later handles JIRA_API_TOKEN and other secrets.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a behavioral test that drives `pytest_sessionfinish` over a constructed session (with jira_live-marked items and an executed-nodeid set) and asserts `session.exitstatus` flips to `TESTS_FAILED` on all-skip, stays unchanged when >=1 executed, and is a no-op when REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL is unset.
evidence: tests/external/conftest.py:94 `jira_items = [it for it in session.items if it.get_closest_marker("jira_live") is not None]`

ABSENCE: `search_files` for `pytest_sessionfinish` shows the hook defined only in tests/external/conftest.py; test_external_canary.py never references or invokes it — the tests only call `_conftest._jira_canary_should_fail(...)` (test_external_canary.py:47-62) and `_conftest.pytest_collection_modifyitems(...)` (lines 92,100).

Ticket 5f39 AC: 'the conftest hook FAILS an external session ... iff >=1 jira_live test was collected but zero executed ... asserted by a behavioral test driving the hook over constructed report objects.'
advisory.finding: The `pytest_sessionfinish` hook — which does the marker-based collected/executed counting and flips `session.exitstatus` to failure — is never exercised by any test; only the pure helper `_jira_canary_should_fail` is tested. The ticket's acceptance criterion explicitly requires 'a behavioral test driving the hook over constructed report objects' asserting the session flips to failure iff >=1 jira_live test collected/zero executed, passes when >=1 executed, and is a no-op when REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL unset.
scenarios: A refactor that mis-scopes the marker query (e.g. filters the wrong marker) or forgets to assign `session.exitstatus` would leave every canary test green while the scheduled canary silently stops failing.
The executed-count computation (`it.nodeid in executed_nodeids`) mis-comparing nodeids would go undetected.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test driving `pytest_runtest_logreport` with constructed TestReport-like objects (call/pass, call/skipped, setup/skipped) asserting only the non-skipped call-phase nodeid lands in the executed set.
evidence: tests/external/conftest.py:75 `if report.when == "call" and not report.skipped:` then `_executed_set(config).add(report.nodeid)`
ABSENCE: `search_files` for `pytest_runtest_logreport` returns only its definition and internal references in tests/external/conftest.py; no test module invokes it.
advisory.finding: `pytest_runtest_logreport` — the hook that discriminates executed from skipped tests via `report.when == 'call' and not report.skipped` and populates the executed-nodeid set that the canary counts against — has no test. This 'ran vs skipped' distinction is the core signal of the canary yet is entirely uncovered.
scenarios: A regression that records setup-phase or skipped reports as 'executed' would mark all-skip runs as having executed tests, so the canary would never fire — a silent defeat with no failing test.

coaching.coaching: File a follow-on ticket (discovered_from this change) for mandatory acli checksum verification, a pre-existing / out-of-scope issue — do not fix it in this change.
coaching.subject: mandatory acli checksum verification
